arts.ttl: Nassef Sawiris handed Aston Villa blow as Premier League release statement
arts.sum: Premier League clubs have today voted to change Associated Party Transaction (APT) rules. A Premier League statement reads: "At a Premier League Shareholders’ meeting today, clubs approved changes to the League’s Associated Party Transaction (APT) rules.

arts.ttl: Premier League clubs approve APT rule changes in blow to Manchester City
arts.sum: Associated party transaction changes approved by 16-4 Aston Villa, Newcastle, Nottingham Forest side with City Premier League clubs approved changes to associated party transaction (APT) rules on Friday in the face of opposition led by Manchester City. The alterations were swiftly approved by 16 votes to four, with Aston Villa, Newcastle and Nottingham Forest standing with City. Clubs were asked at a shareholder meeting in central London to pass modest changes to rules relating to deals when they generate income from sources related to their ownership. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Huge Aston Villa and Man City vote twist as new row threatens to erupt
arts.sum: Sources close to the Black Country club were surprised to be listed as a potential backer of City, given they supported the Premier League when the four-in-a-row champions first launched its legal challenge against the APT rules earlier this year. The Premier League has consulted its clubs for more than a month on APT rule changes since an arbitration tribunal found aspects of them unlawful following City’s challenge.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery a 'man of his word' after blocking Aston Villa transfer
arts.sum: Aston Villa youngster Lamare Bogarde has described Unai Emery as a "football genius" and says he is happy to play across several positions for him. Instead, Bogarde remained in the first-team and made his Premier League debut at the end of August in Villa's 2-1 win over Leicester City at the King Power Stadium.

arts.ttl: Manchester United legends agree about Aston Villa ahead Crystal Palace clash
arts.sum: Opponents Palace beat Villa in the Carabao Cup three weeks ago, but haven't won away from home in the league so far this season and are in need of a result after spending the international break in the bottom three. Former Villa forward Chris Sutton, as well as Manchester United legends Gary Neville and Paul Scholes don't think the Eagles will be able to kick-start their campaign this weekend and are instead backing Emery's side to earn their first win in a month.

arts.ttl: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
arts.sum: Højlund has chance to impress Amorim, Villa look to end miserable month and will Forest trouble Arsenal? After masterminding Leicester’s return to the Premier League, Enzo Maresca jumped ship to Chelsea. The Italian had given little confidence he would stay at the King Power, despite his success amid problems in the background and the lure of London. Maresca did, however, ensure he took one thing with him in the form of the central midfielder Kiernan Dewsbury-Hall, a leading light in the Foxes’ promotion charge. While Maresca has impressed at Stamford Bridge, Dewsbury-Hall is yet to find his feet and has been limited to a cup-player role. Of his 10 appearances for Chelsea, only three have been in the league and none from the start. It might be time to show some faith in him and there is no better place to do so than his old home. Dewsbury-Hall rose through the ranks at Leicester and his £30m transfer fee this summer was highly beneficial to their accounts after financial problems. Leicester fans will welcome back Maresca and Dewsbury-Hall with open arms, the embrace the latter might need to kickstart his Chelsea career. Will Unwin Leicester v Chelsea, Saturday 12.30pm (all times GMT) Bournemouth v Brighton, Saturday 3pm Arsenal v Nottingham Forest, Saturday 3pm Aston Villa v Crystal Palace, Saturday 3pm Everton v Brentford, Saturday 3pm Fulham v Wolves, Saturday 3pm Continue reading...

arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Premier League clash between Aston Villa and Crystal Palace, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa hero names five Celtic players who could walk into Unai Emery's squad
arts.sum: Petrov, who played for both Celtic and Villa in his career, is confident the Hoops will compete well with Unai Emery's side after listing a number of stars who would cut it at the top of the Premier League. Stiliyan Petrov has named five players who wouldn't look out of place in the Aston Villa squad.
